target: Corrected patches for EXP-451 P1 sublabel (find_text missing anchor) and P4 (invented [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] bracket convention). Both patches supersede corresponding EXP-451 patches.
integration_note: When integrating EXP-451: (1) for P1 sublabel_change, use the corrected find_text/replace_text from this EXP which includes the <a href> anchor; (2) for P4, use the corrected replace_text from this EXP which removes the invented [REMOVED]/[SUSPENDED] bracket notation. EXP-451 P1 main text change, P2, P3, P3a, P3b, P5 are unaffected. Note on target_file: EXP-451 targets docs/index.html for P1 main text and P4; the sublabel text for P1 actually lives in data/sections.json (confirmed by grep). The sublabel patch in this EXP targets data/sections.json. The P4 patch targets docs/index.html as EXP-451 specified (the accuracy math is in generate-html.js source but editing docs/index.html directly via find/replace is the decider's existing pattern for EXP-451 patches).

patches.description: Corrected sublabel find_text for EXP-451 P1. The original sublabel_change find_text was missing the <a href='#eg-falsifiability'...> anchor that wraps 'What would change this?' in the actual rendered HTML. Actual text in data/sections.json (confirmed by grep) includes the full anchor tag. Without the correct find_text the patch either fails to match or silently drops the cross-tab link.
patches.find_text: None produces a dome-derived prediction that both differs from standard physics and is confirmed by measurement. What would change this?
patches.replace_text: None produces a dome-derived prediction that survives both internal-consistency and measurement checks. What would change this?

patches.description: Corrected P4 replace_text: removes the invented [REMOVED] and [SUSPENDED] bracket convention. Grep of docs/index.html and raw-text/03-predictions.txt confirms zero occurrences of [REMOVED] and only one SUSPENDED (as a section header, not a bracket-marker). The original replace_text claimed dome 'visibly marked [REMOVED] or [SUSPENDED]' which is factually wrong and hands dome defenders a valid rebuttal ('show me where we use those markers'). Fix: describe what the dome actually does (removes/suspends entries from the predictions page) without inventing bracket notation.
patches.find_text: Computed as 70 confirmed ÷ (70 + 4 acknowledged + 4 silent), where the silent-failure set is the list of claims the dome has visibly removed or suspended but excluded from its own accuracy denominator. See the failure list →
patches.replace_text: Computed as 70 confirmed ÷ (70 + 4 acknowledged + 4 silent), where the silent-failure set is the list of claims the dome has removed from its predictions page or suspended without counting as failures in its own accuracy denominator. Each silent-failure entry is named individually in the failure list with evidence from the dome's public commit history — the criterion is observable, not imputed from absence of result. See the failure list →
